_comment: Editorial current-status facts for the Australia banner. Slow-changing, sourced. The pipeline merges these with counts derived from the live data. Keep the tone calm and factual; human risk is currently low and the page must say so.
headline: H5N1 bird flu (clade 2.3.4.4b) reached Australian wild birds in June 2026.
summary: For the first time, high pathogenicity H5N1 avian influenza has been detected in wild birds in Australia. Detections so far are in migratory seabirds; there are no detections in commercial poultry. The risk to people is low.

first_detection.confirmed_by: CSIRO Australian Centre for Disease Preparedness

human_risk_note: Australia's only human H5N1 case is a single travel-acquired case from May 2024, a different virus lineage unrelated to the 2026 wildlife detections. No human-to-human transmission has been reported.
poultry_note: No detections in commercial poultry or other agriculture to date. The 2024 to 2025 outbreaks in poultry were a different subtype (H7) and are resolved.

report.label: Report sick or dead wild birds

caveats: "First detection" refers to Australian wild birds. A separate possible incursion at sub-Antarctic Heard Island, an external territory, was reported in late 2025 and is not counted in these figures.
Not every Western Australian detection is itemised in public data; the map shows the events published by official sources.
